task_completion.description: Verify that three new mass components (Atmos Sensor, Bio Sample, Dummy Payload) have been added to the Payload section of the rocket design.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket design tree, under the Payload section, there should be three distinct mass components named 'Atmos Sensor', 'Bio Sample', and 'Dummy Payload'.

task_completion.description: Verify that three flight configurations (Mission Alpha, Mission Beta, Mission Gamma) have been created using the Aerotech J420R motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Flight configurations' or 'Simulations' tab, there are three configurations named 'Mission Alpha', 'Mission Beta', and 'Mission Gamma', and the motor configuration shows 'J420R'.

task_completion.description: Verify that component overrides are used to assign only the specific payload to each mission.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the flight configuration edit window, the 'Component overrides' tab should show specific mass components being checked (included) or unchecked (excluded) for the respective missions.

task_completion.description: Verify that simulations have been run for all three configurations, producing apogee results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Simulations tab shows simulation results (such as Apogee and Velocity) or a green checkmark for Mission Alpha, Mission Beta, and Mission Gamma.

task_completion.description: Verify that the modified rocket design is saved as multimission_payload.ork in the ~/Documents/rockets/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title bar displays 'multimission_payload.ork', or a file explorer/terminal shows the file in the ~/Documents/rockets/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that a text report named mission_matrix_report.txt is created in ~/Documents/exports/ containing the simulated apogee altitudes for each mission.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displays the contents of 'mission_matrix_report.txt' in ~/Documents/exports/, showing the recorded apogee altitudes for the three missions.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the OpenRocket GUI to configure the payloads, flight configurations, and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenRocket application open with the agent interacting with the component tree, flight configurations, and simulation tabs.

integrity.description: Verify that the apogee altitudes recorded in the text report match the actual simulation results displayed in the OpenRocket GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The apogee values typed into the mission_matrix_report.txt file should correspond exactly to the simulated apogee values visible in the OpenRocket Simulations tab.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not simply create the report file with fabricated numbers without running the simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of the simulations actually being executed in OpenRocket before the report file is created and populated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires saving the rocket design to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/multimission_payload.ork and outputting a report at /home/ga/Documents/exports/mission_matrix_report.txt. The required payload masses are: Mission Alpha (Atmos Sensor) = 150g (0.15kg), Mission Beta (Bio Sample) = 300g (0.3kg), and Mission Gamma (Dummy Payload) = 50g (0.05kg). The pass threshold for the verifier is 65 points.
